name: Willow Moon
description: Willow Moon is a sacred herb within Celtic tradition, deeply intertwined with lunar cycles, feminine energy, and the Otherworld. It is said to bloom only under the light of the full moon, its silvery leaves shimmering with an ethereal glow. The plant is believed to possess potent magical properties, particularly related to divination, healing, and emotional well-being. Celtic druids and healers utilized Willow Moon in rituals to connect with the spirit world, gain insight into the future, and soothe troubled minds. Its association with the moon links it to intuition, dreams, and the subconscious, making it a valuable tool for self-reflection and understanding. Willow Moon is considered a sacred offering to deities associated with water, the moon, and fertility. The herb's calming and restorative properties were also used to create poultices and teas for physical ailments, especially those related to women's health. Symbolically, Willow Moon represents adaptability, grace, and the ability to navigate life's challenges with resilience. Its presence in Celtic folklore and mythology highlights the reverence for nature and the interconnectedness of the physical and spiritual realms. It is a reminder of the power of intuition and the importance of embracing change. The subtle fragrance is said to induce prophetic dreams. The plant is exceptionally rare, growing only near ancient standing stones and sacred groves.
subtitle: Herb of Lunar Magic and Healing

keyMyths.title: The Willow Moon Prophecy
keyMyths.description: A legend tells of a seer who drank a Willow Moon infusion and foresaw a great threat to the Celtic lands, allowing the people to prepare and defend themselves. This myth highlights the plant's divinatory powers and its importance in protecting the community.

sources.author: Miranda Green
sources.description: Provides information on Celtic rituals and beliefs surrounding sacred plants.
sources.text: Provides information on Celtic rituals and beliefs surrounding sacred plants.
